summary: We recommend Cloudflare Codemode as the Open Outcome MCP server pattern because wrangler dev serves a streamable HTTP endpoint on localhost:8787 with a single code tool whose description carries the typed verifier surface. Calling the code tool with a JavaScript snippet that awaits codemode.verify_outcome_pair returns a Verdict whose overall and dimension scores match the Python verifier bit-for-bit on the same inputs. The tradeoff is one extra dependency (@cloudflare/codemode plus a Worker Loader binding) in exchange for ~1k tokens of schema regardless of how many verifier methods we expose.

claims.statement: wrangler dev serves a streamable HTTP MCP endpoint on localhost:8787 with the LOADER worker_loaders binding wired up.

citations.title: wrangler.jsonc declares worker_loaders binding LOADER and nodejs_compat

claims.caveats: Verified locally on macOS with wrangler 4.93.1 against Node 24.

claims.statement: tools/list returns exactly one code tool whose description carries the typed verifier surface generated by @cloudflare/codemode.

citations.title: src/index.ts wraps inner McpServer with codeMcpServer

claims.caveats: The inner native MCP tools (verify_outcome_pair, spec_info) are not exposed directly; agents call them via the single code tool.

claims.statement: Calling the code tool with await codemode.verify_outcome_pair returns a Verdict that matches the Python verifier on the same outcome+report pair.

citations.title: TypeScript port of HeuristicVerifier in this repo

claims.caveats: Determinism verified locally against the opensubagents/outcomes outcome-gate bootstrap pair (overall 5.0) and a deliberately weak pair (overall 2.2).

open_questions: Should the v1 deploy live at outcomes.mcp.opensubagents.org with OAuth, or as a personal-account Worker accessed via Claude Code MCP client config?
methodology_notes: Verified locally on 2026-05-22 by running wrangler dev, calling POST /mcp tools/list to confirm a single code tool, and calling tools/call code with two snippets — the opensubagents/outcomes bootstrap pair (overall 5.0) and a deliberately weak pair (overall 2.2). Both verdicts matched the Python verifier.
